# Referee

Referee is founder-led research-integrity infrastructure for scholarly publishing. The platform turns technical evidence into transparent, machine-readable reliability signals that support structured triage and targeted expert review at high submission volume.

Referee does not claim to replace expert peer review with a black box. It organizes checks into a structured evaluation record with explicit flaw categories, unresolved items, and auditable outputs.

## Key routes
- / — Homepage with product positioning, workflow, audience, differentiation, founder context, and demo call to action.
- /about — Mission, leadership profile, advisor profile, and collaboration/contact invitations.
- /blog — Index of published long-form articles.
- /blog/{slug} — Individual published blog articles.
- /faq — Frequently asked questions about model assumptions, incentives, adoption, AI, and governance concerns.
- /talks — Recorded presentations and interviews about the project.

## Homepage coverage
- Problem framing: rising submission volume, limited intake review capacity, fragmented integrity checks, and low auditability.
- Solution framing: structured screening and triage with transparent reliability scoring tied to evidence.
- Workflow model (CRWE-based):
  1) ingest paper evidence
  2) map checks to explicit weakness categories
  3) record checked vs unresolved items
  4) output machine-readable reliability signals for triage
- Value claims: better triage, better allocation of expert reviewer time, and clearer documentation of research risk.
- Target users: conference organizers, publishers/journals, and research organizations.
- Positioning: infrastructure layer that unifies checks into a reusable record rather than isolated one-off tools.
- Current demo scope: a live dashboard and paper-checking workflow are available today; false positives and coverage depth are still being improved.
- Founder context: founder-owned technical architecture and implementation.

## About coverage
- Mission: improve transparency, efficiency, and accountability in research evaluation through reliability-oriented infrastructure.
- Leadership: Erik Schneider (Founder & Executive Director), background spanning finance, cybersecurity, insider risk, and computing.
- Advisor: Dr. Marcus Thomas (computational scientist; immuno-oncology/personalized cancer vaccine pipeline expertise).
- Collaboration posture: active outreach for additional qualified advisors and collaborators.

## Blog coverage (published posts)
- /blog/academic-collegiality-is-a-bug-not-a-feature
  - Title: Academic collegiality is a bug, not a feature (and it's largely not true)
  - Focus: critiques assumptions about collegial peer review and argues for more direct, democratized, adversarial criticism in research evaluation.
- /blog/gaming-referee
  - Title: Gaming Referee
  - Focus: living threat model describing how adversaries might game the system (collusion, sybils, flooding, corruption, etc.) and corresponding mitigations.
- /blog/no-ai-agents-no-scalability-in-research-validation
  - Title: No AI Agents, No Scalability in Research Validation
  - Focus: argues that scalable research validation requires specialized AI agents paired with validation controls and expert oversight.

## FAQ topic coverage
- Journal adoption skepticism and incentives to participate.
- Relationship to existing post-publication commentary systems (for example, PubPeer and related ecosystems).
- Handling non-binary or subjective weakness assessments.
- Cross-disciplinary taxonomy standardization challenges.
- Limits and usefulness of a single reliability score.
- Adversarial/gaming behavior and why it is expected.
- Participation by non-academics and credential boundary concerns.
- Resistance to public criticism of research flaws.
- AI and bot roles in both detection and evaluation workflows.
- Anonymity, accountability, and abuse mitigation.
- Funding sustainability for incentives and operations.
- Career and morale implications of transparent flaw exposure.
- Variability in research interpretation and unresolved subjectivity.

## Talks coverage
- The Referee Project: An Update with Sebastián Buriticá Arias (2024)
  - Focus: bug-bounty-inspired review model, reliability scoring, adversarial process design, and current MVP direction.
- The Referee Project at DeSci Labs Forum (2023)
  - Focus: taking the peer out of peer review, maker/breaker role separation, transparent scoring, incentives, and AI/human review dynamics.

## Public API surface
- POST /api/newsletter
  - Purpose: newsletter subscription endpoint.
  - Request body: `email` (required), `firstName` (optional).
  - Behavior: validates input, submits double-opt-in signup request, returns JSON status/message.

## Notes for machine consumers
- Canonical human-facing route inventory is based on current App Router pages and navigation.
- Blog inventory above reflects currently published posts in the repository content store.
- Legacy content entries that are not represented as current App Router routes are intentionally excluded from canonical page inventory.
